Title: Tabung Haji RCI report delay: Anwar cites confidence concerns
Article:
PORT DICKSON: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the government did not release the Royal Commission of Inquiry (RCI) report on Tabung Haji three years ago because doing so at the time could have triggered a crisis of confidence that might have led to the institution's collapse.
Speaking on the issue, Anwar said the government believed that publishing the report then could have caused depositors to lose confidence in Tabung Haji and withdraw their savings, threatening the viability of the pilgrimage fund.
"But people criticised me, asking why I had not released the report. I said that three years ago, we could not have made it public.
"If we had released it then, the public would have lost confidence in Tabung Haji. They would have withdrawn their funds, and Tabung Haji as an institution could have collapsed," he said in his speech at the Semarak Kenegaraan programme held at the Army Basic Training Centre (Pusasda) here.
Present were Deputy Defence Minister Adly Zahari, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar and Armed Forces chief General Tan Sri Malek Razak Sulaiman.
Anwar said the circumstances had since changed following reforms to the fund's management.
He said Tabung Haji's leadership had been overhauled over the past three years, with Tan Sri Abdul Rashid Hussain appointed as chairman.
He described Rashid as having an outstanding management track record and said the institution had begun to regain its footing.
Yesterday, Bersama leader and former economy minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli said in a statement that Anwar had delayed the release of the RCI report on Tabung Haji to maintain Umno's suppor.
He said the report should have been made public in 2023, when it was completed, as its findings involved matters of public interest.
In 2022, the then Yang di-Pertuan Agong, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, consented to the establishment of the RCI to investigate Tabung Haji's management and operational issues between 2014 and 2020.
Earlier yesterday, Anwar, after opening a forum at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur, announced that the government may release the final report of the RCI into Tabung Haji.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor police arrest three, seize 71 bitcoin miners in TNB power theft bust
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor police have busted a syndicate illegally mining Bitcoin using electricity stolen from Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB), following four raids on July 22 and 23.
Police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the operation, codenamed Op Letrik, was carried out by the Johor police headquarters' Criminal Investigation Department (D4), working alongside TNB's Southern Region SEAL team.
He said three local men, aged between 26 and 46, were arrested in Iskandar Puteri, Johor Baru Utara and Kulai for their suspected involvement in the Bitcoin mining operation.
During a press conference at the Johor police headquarters today, he said the items seized included 71 cryptocurrency mining machines, two computers, two laptops, five routers, two monitors, two computer keyboards, a mobile phone and two vehicles.
Ab Rahaman said initial investigations revealed the syndicate had been operating for about a month before it was dismantled, following intelligence gathering by police and TNB.
He said the syndicate is believed to have used direct tapping connections that bypassed legitimate TNB electricity meters, allowing their Bitcoin mining operations to run without paying bills.
He added that TNB's checks estimated the utility company's losses from the illegal cryptocurrency mining at the four premises to be RM67,502.30 over the roughly one-month period.
The four premises raided, comprising three residential homes and one shop-house, were rented at RM5,000 to RM6,000 per month each, and police are still investigating those behind the rental arrangements.
He said one of the suspects arrested is believed to have acted as the manager for all four premises, while the other two men are suspected to be external technicians tasked with managing the wiring and Bitcoin mining machine installations.
"Initial investigations found the syndicate was capable of generating profits of between RM80,000 and RM100,000 per month, while the suspects are believed to have been paid around RM5,000 a month," he said.
"All three suspects have been remanded until July 26, and we are actively tracking down several other individuals believed to be linked to the syndicate," he said.
The case is being investigated under Section 427 of the Penal Code for committing mischief, which carries a jail term of between one and five years, or a fine, or both, upon conviction.
It is also being investigated under Section 37(1) of the Electricity Supply Act 1990 for interfering with or altering electrical installations, which carries a fine not exceeding RM100,000, or imprisonment of not more than five years, or both, upon conviction.
Between January last year and last month, the Johor Contingent Police raided 16 premises believed to be involved in illegal cryptocurrency mining, seizing various equipment including 158 mining machines.
"TNB's estimated losses from these activities during that period amounted to nearly RM1 million," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UK retail sales unexpectedly rise 1pct in June
Article:
LONDON: British retail sales volumes unexpectedly rose by 1.0 per cent in monthly terms in June, according to official figures published on Friday.
Economists polled by Reuters had mostly expected a monthly fall of 0.3 per cent in sales volumes.
Compared with a year earlier, volumes were 4.2 per cent higher, against economists' expectations of a 2.3 per cent rise.
Recent updates from major British retailers have painted a mixed picture of consumer demand.
Supermarket group Sainsbury's said trading in its third quarter had improved, helped by the heatwave, while electricals retailer Currys reported "very solid" trading, with the soccer World Cup spurring demand for televisions and the hot weather causing a spike in demand for fans and air conditioning products.
However, Sports Direct owner Frasers warned it was still feeling the impact of tough trading conditions, subdued consumer confidence and industry-wide excess inventory levels.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: N9 early polling centres to be closed in stages
Article:
SEREMBAN: Early polling centres for the 16th Negri Sembilan state election, which open at 8am on July 28, will be closed in stages between noon and 5pm.
According to a Federal Government Gazette dated July 23 regarding the polling hours for the state election, 18 out of 38 early polling centres will close at 12pm.
These involve one centre each in the Chennah, Pertang, Jeram Padang, Lenggeng, Nilai, Seri Menanti, Johol, Labu, Rahang, Rantau, Kota, Chuah, Lukut, Bagan Pinang, Linggi, Gemas, and Gemencheh state constituencies, covering polling at district police headquarters, police stations, and the 508th Army Reserve Regiment at Jalan Rasah Camp.
Early polling centres in Bahau and one in Paroi will close at 2pm, while 18 others will close at 5pm.
The 5pm closures cover one centre each in Repah, Gemas, Sri Tanjung, Linggi, Rantau, Chembong, Paroi, Rahang, Bukit Kepayang, Pilah, Sikamat, Temiang, Nilai, Serting, Klawang and Sungai Lui, plus two centres in Bagan Pinang.
Early voting will involve 16,884 military personnel and their spouses, as well as 5,455 policemen.
On polling day, Aug 1, a total of 395 polling centres will open from 8am to 6pm, utilising schools, community halls, colleges, sports complexes and public halls for the election of new state representatives.
A total of 867,151 regular voters are eligible to cast their ballots in this state election.
The Negri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly was officially dissolved on June 5, with the consent of the Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir.
In the 15th state election in 2023, Pakatan Harapan (PH) won 17 of the 36 state seats, followed by Barisan Nasional (BN) with 14, and Perikatan Nasional with five.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Boy, 5, dies with bruises; mother and boyfriend held
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A 5-year-old boy was found dead with bruises on his body at a rented apartment in Gelugor, prompting police to arrest his mother and her boyfriend.
The Indonesian woman, 30, and her Bangladeshi boyfriend, 34, who told police they were husband and wife, were later remanded for seven days to assist investigations into the child's death.
The remand order against the couple was issued by magistrate Nadratun Naim Mohd Saidi until July 30 to facilitate investigations under Section 31(1)(a) of the Child Act 2001.
Earlier today, the suspects were brought separately to the magistrate's court in handcuffs.
It is understood the incident came to light after the unemployed Bangladeshi man discovered the child vomiting at their rented apartment in Gelugor at about 10am yesterday.
He then took the boy to a nearby clinic for treatment while the child's mother was at work.
However, the child reportedly lost consciousness at the clinic before being transferred to Penang Hospital, where he was pronounced dead.
Hospital staff later lodged a police report after discovering bruises across the child's body.
Following the report, police arrested the woman, who works as a restaurant assistant, and her boyfriend to assist in investigations.
The boy was understood to be the son of the Indonesian woman and her Rohingya husband, who died last year.
Police are awaiting the post-mortem report to determine whether the external injuries were linked to the child's death before deciding on further action.
The New Straits Times has reached out to state police chief Datuk Dennis Lim for comment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Chong Wei visits US Embassy ahead of Los Angeles Games preparations
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Badminton legend Datuk Seri Lee Chong Wei, a three-time Olympic silver medallist and former world number one, visited the United States Embassy in Kuala Lumpur ahead of his upcoming trip to Los Angeles.
Chong Wei is Badminton Association of Malaysia performance committee chairman.
The visit was shared on the embassy's Instagram account, which is marking the United States' 250th anniversary of independence this year with a nationwide "Freedom 250" campaign.
"It was a privilege to welcome Malaysian badminton legend Datuk Seri Lee Chong Wei to the US Embassy," the embassy said in its post, adding that few athletes have inspired more Malaysians through sport than Lee.
The embassy said sports have a unique way of bringing people together across borders, and that it was proud to support Lee ahead of his trip to Los Angeles.
Lee had previously urged the Badminton Association of Malaysia to
begin preparations early for the 2028 Los Angeles Game
.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hannah Yeoh likens BN-PN pact to Titanic sinking
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The cooperation between Barisan Nasional and Perikatan Nasional is like the Titanic waiting to sink, DAP deputy secretary-general Hannah Yeoh said.
She said the outcome was inevitable as the partnership was not built on trust or loyalty.
While the collaboration might appear strong for now, she added, it was unlikely to last because both sides harboured conflicting political ambitions.
"I liken their cooperation to the Titanic, which can sink at any moment.
"They cheered with joy at the start of their voyage, only to sink in the end after hitting a massive iceberg they failed to see right in front of them," Yeoh said in a Facebook post.
BN and PN have reached an electoral understanding for the Aug 1 Negri Sembilan election to avoid clashing across all 36 seats.
They will face PH, which is contesting all 36 seats, and Bersatu, which has fielded candidates in 24 seats.
Yeoh said both BN and PN harboured ambitions to lead the federal government.
"Each side aspires to become the prime minister and will step on one another to rise to the top.
"They also do not want their partners to succeed. They only want to elevate their own top leaders. That is the analogy for the BN-PN cooperation today," she said.
In contrast, she said, PH had proven the loyalty of its component parties, with PKR, DAP and Amanah consistently supporting Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
"We remain steadfast with only one captain at the helm. Ensuring a victory for PH candidates is a symbol of defending the value of political friendship.
"A victory for PH candidates in the Negri Sembilan election is vital to defend the values of loyalty and political friendship that form the cornerstone of our coalition.
"These are values absent in any political alliance other than PH. Keep PH, and you will maintain a government and leaders who remain loyal to this value of political friendship. Believe me," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Motorist swerves to avoid animal, plunges car into Sungai Langat
Article:
KAJANG: A 56-year-old driver escaped unhurt after his car plunged into Sungai Langat when he swerved to avoid an animal in Hulu Langat yesterday.
Kajang police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said the man was driving from Sungai Congkak to Nilai when he spotted an animal crossing the road near Institut Kemahiran Belia Negara.
"His attempt to avoid the animal caused him to lose control of the vehicle before it skidded and fell into Sungai Langat," Naazron said in a statement.
He said two similar accidents had been reported in the area and advised motorists to exercise greater caution along the route.
"If they spot an animal or any obstruction on the road, drivers should gradually slow down and avoid manoeuvres which could lead them to lose control of the vehicle," Naazron said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SK Bukit Pantai gets rebuilt classroom block after termite damage
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: SK Bukit Pantai in Bangsar today received the keys to its newly rebuilt Block C, replacing the original structure that suffered extensive termite-related structural damage over several years.
Lembah Pantai MP Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil said the block's rebuilding project had been delayed by the Covid-19 pandemic but has now been completed.
"Alhamdulillah, today we can see these classrooms are ready for use again, allowing pupils to learn in a much better environment," he told reporters after the handover ceremony for Block C at the school today, which was also attended by Kuala Lumpur Education Department director Megat Affandi Ismail.
Also present was SK Bukit Pantai headmaster Shaharuddin Eusop.
Fahmi, who is also Communications Minister, thanked Public Works Department-appointed contractor Picoland Sdn Bhd for completing the project on schedule and delivering quality work.
He said the new block houses six classrooms for Year One and Year Two pupils, bringing an end to the floating classroom system previously adopted by the school.
Fahmi said that the open space between the two school blocks would also be considered for upgrading works, including the installation of artificial turf to create a recreational area for pupils.
Meanwhile, he announced that a new primary school, SK Seri Pantai, will be built on the former site of SMK Seri Pantai in Kampung Kerinchi, Pantai Dalam.
Demolition works are expected to begin within the next few months before construction of the new school starts.
Fahmi said the project is being planned to cater for population growth driven by new housing developments in the Lembah Pantai area.
"SK Bangsar, SK Bukit Bandaraya and SK Bukit Pantai are currently operating at full capacity. SK Bangsar alone has more than 1,800 pupils.
"We want to ensure a more conducive learning environment. The proposed new school is expected to have at least 36 classrooms, although the final details are still being reviewed," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: School van driver charged over sexual assault of 8-year-old
Article:
BUTTERWORTH: A 60-year-old school van driver pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court to sexually assaulting an 8-year-old girl last week.
The accused entered the plea after the charge was read to him before Judge Roslan Hamid.
He was charged with committing physical sexual assault against the girl by rubbing her genitals while she was sleeping in the front seat of the van in Tasek Gelugor on the evening of July 15.
The charge was framed under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act, which carries a maximum jail term of 20 years and whipping upon conviction.
Deputy Public Prosecutor Nadhirah Abdul Rahim prosecuted, while the accused was represented by lawyer Muhammad Nur Arif.
The prosecution did not propose bail as the offence involved a child victim.
"However, we seek additional conditions should bail be granted, including that the accused surrender his passport, report to the nearest police station once a month, and refrain from approaching the victim and prosecution witnesses," she said.
In mitigation, Nur Arif said the accused, a father of seven, had cooperated with the police and supported his wife and a 21-year-old child.
He added that his client was not a flight risk and that the man's wife and child were present in court to act as sureties.
Roslan allowed bail at RM10,000 with one surety and imposed the additional conditions requested by the prosecution.
The court fixed Sept 1 for case mention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM500 aid for Penang padi, vegetable farmers
Article:
KEPALA BATAS: The Penang government has approved a one-off RM500 Bantuan Sara Hidup for 6,572 eligible padi and vegetable farmers in the state, involving an allocation of RM3.29 million.
Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the assistance, which will benefit 5,567 padi farmers and 1,005 vegetable farmers, is part of the state government's efforts to strengthen the food security agenda.
"Payment of the aid will be made in the near future based on the list of farmers registered with the Penang State Agriculture Department," he told reporters after visiting the Penang branch of the Malaysian Agricultural Research and Development Institute (Mardi) in Pinang Tunggal here today.
Also present were State Rural Development, Agrotechnology, Food Security and Entrepreneurship Committee chairman Datuk Rashidi Zinol and Mardi deputy director-general (Development and Operations) Dr Sanimah Simoh.
Chow said the assistance also fulfils the state government's commitment under the Penang Unity Manifesto 2023, particularly Theme 10: Food Supply Chain Security, which emphasises cost of living assistance for padi farmers, vegetable growers and fishermen.
He said Penang has 11,858 hectares of active padi cultivation areas farmed by 5,567 farmers, with an average yield of 4.69 tonnes per hectare as of this year, while several areas have recorded exceptionally high padi yields.
For the vegetable farming sector, Chow said 1,005 farmers produced 10,648 tonnes of vegetables last year, underscoring the important role of the farming community in ensuring adequate food supplies for the state.
"To date, Penang has established 384 community and urban gardens out of the target of 500 by 2030, and this achievement reflects the state government's commitment to modernising the agricultural sector sustainably through technology.
"In realising this agenda, Mardi continues to be the state government's strategic partner through the development of technology and the transfer of knowledge to farmers, padi growers, livestock breeders, fishermen and agro-food entrepreneurs," he said.
He said the state government would continue strengthening the agro-food sector through various initiatives, including expanding the use of technology and modern farming practices, enhancing productivity, and reinforcing strategic cooperation with the Penang State Agriculture Department, Mardi and related agencies.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Over 100 Rohingya families identified in Ketereh
Article:
KOTA BARU: More than 100 Rohingya families have been identified as living at several locations in Ketereh for the past several years.
Kelantan Immigration Department director Garry Belayong John Siki said Ketereh remained the largest Rohingya settlement in the state to date.
He said investigations and intelligence gathered by the department showed that the community had been living in the area for many years.
He said the department had been continuously monitoring the settlement and would step up enforcement and surveillance from time to time.
"Based on our investigations, more than 100 Rohingya families are living at several locations in Ketereh, making it the largest Rohingya settlement in Kelantan.
"We have been conducting regular monitoring and intelligence operations in the area and will continue carrying out enforcement and surveillance from time to time," he said.
He said the Immigration Department would continue carrying out inspections in every district across the state in collaboration with relevant local authorities.
"We will continue conducting regular inspections and work closely with local municipal councils to check on undocumented migrants and ensure compliance with immigration laws," he said.
He said the department remained committed to strengthening enforcement efforts to curb immigration offences and monitor areas identified as migrant hotspots in the state.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 11 men charged as members of organised crime syndicate 'Vinod Gang'
Article:
SEPANG: Eleven men were brought before the Sessions Court here today on charges of being members of an organised crime syndicate known as Vinod Gang.
No plea was recorded after the charges were read before judge Husna Dzulkifly, as the case falls under the jurisdiction of the High Court.
All 11 are accused of being members of the syndicate in Bandar Rimbayu, Telok Panglima Garang, between April 2024 and June 27, 2026, under Section 130V(1) of the Penal Code.
They are Muhammad Vinod Saktivel, 30, believed to be the group's leader; M Puvaneswaran, 34; Lawrence Alfred, 34; G Sanggeethan, 31; M Naviedran, 29; and S Sri Tharan, 27; M Surenthar, 27; N A N Viknesvaran, 27; J M Kishaan, 24; R Shervinkumar, 23; and T Saarvin, 21.
Deputy public prosecutors Chow Siang Kong and Muhammad Syahrezal Mohd Shukri appeared for the prosecution, while the accused were represented by lawyers T. Mishant and S Devan.
The court set Sept 14 for the next mention of the case.
Earlier, all 11 accused arrived at the court compound around 9am under heavy security from the police's Criminal Investigation Department (CID).
They had been arrested on June 23 and 24 through Op Rapido, a joint operation led by Bukit Aman CID in collaboration with its Selangor counterpart.
In a statement yesterday, Bukit Aman CID director Datuk M. Kumar said the group is believed to have been active since 2023 and involved in gang robberies, attempted murder, armed rioting, causing injury with weapons and mischief.
He said the group's modus operandi was to commit armed robberies using machetes or sharp weapons, targeting residential premises to gain material profit, power or influence.
The arrest of the 11 people has led to the solving of 21 cases and the crippling of one active armed gang robbery syndicate in Selangor.
Police are actively tracking down five other individuals believed to be members of the group, who remain at large.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MMA urges stronger workplace protections after Sabah hospital allegations
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Medical Association (MMA) has called for protection of complainants and witnesses, as well as strict confidentiality, during investigations into allegations of sexual harassment and workplace bullying.
Its president Datuk Dr Thirunavukarasu Rajoo said action must be taken based on the findings without fear or favour, with existing reporting and protection mechanisms reviewed to ensure complaints are handled independently and effectively.
He said MMA viewed seriously the allegations of sexual harassment and workplace bullying involving a public hospital director in Sabah that came to light recently.
"MMA notes that police have commenced an investigation and the Sabah State Health Department has confirmed that a detailed investigation by a special committee appointed by the Health Ministry has been completed.
"Follow-up action is now underway in accordance with the applicable procedures and laws," he said in a statement.
Yesterday, Sabah Police Commissioner Datuk Jauteh Dikun said police were investigating allegations of sexual harassment involving the director after receiving a report in January.
He said the alleged incident took place in October last year but only came to public attention after a video featuring Kepayan assemblyman Chin Tek Ming and Warisan member Dr Istefan Koh went viral on Tuesday.
In the video, they urged the authorities to conduct a thorough investigation into allegations of sexual harassment and workplace bullying involving staff at a hospital in Kota Kinabalu, and to give the matter immediate attention.
Dr Thirunavukarasu said the latest case was not the first workplace safety concern reported within Sabah's public healthcare system.
In 2024, he said MMA had highlighted allegations of prolonged bullying, verbal abuse, threats and pressure to disregard standard operating procedures involving another public hospital unit, which resulted in 11 medical officers leaving the unit.
"The two cases are separate and must be treated based on their own facts.
"However, they highlight the same wider concern: healthcare professionals need a safe working environment and confidence that complaints will be handled fairly and effectively," he said.
He said allegations involving abuse of authority, sexual harassment or workplace bullying must be investigated independently, fairly and within a reasonable timeframe.
"Complainants and witnesses must also be protected from intimidation or retaliation," he said.
He added that a toxic workplace could affect staff morale, patient safety and workforce retention.
"Malaysia cannot invest heavily in training doctors and nurses, only to lose them because the working environment has become unsafe or intolerable.
"Recruitment alone will not solve our workforce challenges if these push factors are not addressed," he said.
He added that MMA remained ready to work with the Health Ministry to strengthen workplace safety, accountability and trust across the healthcare system.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BN-PN understanding is no charade, says Hadi
Article:
MARANG: Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang has dismissed claims that cooperation between Barisan Nasional and Perikatan Nasional in the Negri Sembilan election is a charade.
He said both coalitions had demonstrated their commitment throughout the campaign period.
"The understanding between both coalitions is not a charade," he said after Friday prayers at Masjid Rusila.
"We spent money... Pas members went out to campaign on their own dime, giving it their all. Umno's machinery did the same."
The Marang member of parliament commended the unity shown by the respective campaign machineries.
"Joint ceramahs are being organised at various levels, proving that this unity is real," he said.
"It is very good, very good. I will also be going down to speak next week."
Commenting on the split between Pas and Umno that ended the short-lived Muafakat Nasional pact, Hadi said phases of "marriage and divorce" in politics are normal.
He said while differences of opinion occurred, there were no policy disagreements between the two parties.
"Pas' struggle is based on Islam, while Umno's struggle centres on the Malays. Malays and Islam are inseparable. We only differ in approach, not policy," he said.
On the future sustainability of ties between both parties, Hadi said he hoped that cooperation would continue for the sake of the people and the nation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Teen couple plead guilty to imitation pistol, identity card charges
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: An 18-year-old couple pleaded guilty in separate cases in the Magistrate's Court here today to possessing an imitation pistol and another person's identity card.
Tharmindraa admitted to possessing an imitation metal pistol at 942-H, Halaman Paya Terubong, Mukim 13, Relau, at 12.35am on July 21.
The charge, under Section 36(1) of the Arms Act 1960, carries a maximum penalty of one year's imprisonment, a fine of up to RM5,000, or both upon conviction.
Deputy Public Prosecutor Lau Shavin applied for a social report as the accused had only recently turned 18.
Lawyer Jeffrey Beh, appearing under the National Legal Aid Foundation, said his client was neither working nor studying.
He sought the lowest possible bail, adding that the teenager's mother, who works at a petrol station and earns about RM1,000 a month, was present to stand as surety.
Magistrate S. Selvaranjini allowed bail at RM3,000 with one surety and fixed Aug 20 for mention pending the social report.
In a separate case before the same magistrate, Tharmindraa's girlfriend, S. Jivitha Sri, also pleaded guilty to possessing another person's identity card without reasonable excuse.
She was charged with committing the offence at Room 318-1, Blue Orchid Hotel, Jalan Tun Dr Awang, Bayan Lepas, at about 8pm on July 21.
The charge was framed under Regulation 25(1)(o) of the National Registration Regulations 1990, punishable under Regulation 25(1) of the same regulations, which provides for a jail term of up to three years, a fine of up to RM20,000, or both upon conviction.
Beh urged the court to grant minimal bail, saying Jivitha had previously worked at a convenience store.
"Also, Tharmindraa's mother is willing to stand as Jivitha's surety as her own parents are not in court today," he said.
Selvaranjini, however, asked Beh to explain to his client that her parents needed to be present in court during the next mention for the social report.
She then deferred sentencing to obtain a social report in view of her age.
Selvaranjini allowed bail at RM1,500 with one surety and also fixed Aug 20 for mention.
On July 22, the couple were remanded for four days to assist investigations into an alleged criminal intimidation case involving a pistol-like object.
The arrests followed reports lodged by two men who alleged they were threatened by the male suspect, who was renting a room at their house in Halaman Paya Terubong.
The two men had reportedly reprimanded the couple for arguing with each other before the suspect allegedly pointed the
pistol-like object
at them and left the rented room with his girlfriend.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah mulls ban on endangered mameng
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: Sabah is open to introducing a ban or formulating a policy to protect the endangered Humphead Wrasse, subject to the findings of a resource assessment study.
Sabah Fisheries Department director Datin Dr Shuhadah Mustapha said although the species, locally known as mameng, can currently be caught, sold and consumed within Sabah, the government is willing to consider stronger conservation measures.
The species is listed under Appendix II of the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora (CITES), which regulates international trade.
Shuhadah said Sabah already imposes a zero export quota on the species as a conservation measure.
"In Sabah, the species is not permitted to be exported or traded outside the state, with an export quota of zero imposed as a control measure to ensure its sustainability and conservation.
"However, domestic trade of the Humphead Wrasse within Sabah is currently not prohibited, and the species may be caught, sold, consumed and cultured in cages," she told the New Straits Times.
She added that the department does not issue import or export permits for live Humphead Wrasse, including to Peninsular Malaysia, Sarawak and Labuan.
The department also does not have population data for the species in its natural habitat.
However, 2025 fish landing records showed that 12,090kg of Humphead Wrasse worth RM1.45 million were landed in Semporna, while 36kg valued at RM3,600 were landed in Kota Kinabalu.
The issue recently drew attention after environmental news portal Macaranga reported that the species, which is banned for consumption in China, was being eaten in large quantities in Semporna.
Posts on Chinese social media platform Xiaohongshu also showed users claiming they could eat the fish cheaply in Semporna despite it being illegal in China. In Hong Kong, the fish reportedly fetched US$850 (about RM3,500) per kg in 2019. In Semporna, live Humphead Wrasse sells for between RM150 and RM300 per kg, while dead fish can be bought for about RM50 per kg.
On allegations that cyanide is used to catch the species, Shuhadah said the department has no specific scientific data confirming coral reef damage or mortality resulting from cyanide fishing for Humphead Wrasse.
"However, the use of poison in fishing activities has the potential to cause serious harm to marine ecosystems and constitutes an offence under existing laws."
She said every complaint is investigated under the Fisheries Act 1985 (Amendment 2025) and the Sabah Inland Fisheries and Aquaculture Enactment 2003.
Between 2021 and 2025, the department carried out 1,224 land and sea enforcement operations involving surveillance, intelligence gathering and inspections to curb fisheries offences, including poison fishing.
Fishing with poison or explosives is a non-compoundable offence. Offenders face fines of up to RM250,000 or imprisonment of up to five years under the Fisheries Act 1985 (Amendment 2025).
They may also face prosecution under Sections 25 and 26 of the Sabah Inland Fisheries and Aquaculture Enactment 2003 for selling or possessing fish caught using poison or explosives.
Shuhadah said the department also conducts annual engagement sessions and awareness workshops on endangered marine species involving commercial and traditional fishermen, as well as fish vendors.
This year, three workshops were held in Semporna, Sandakan and Lahad Datu, with plans to expand the programme to include restaurant operators and to distribute awareness posters at restaurants and tourism centres.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia opposes Israel's actions, not Jewish people, says Anwar
Article:
PORT DICKSON: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim reiterated Malaysia's stance against Zionist Israel, while emphasising that the country is not against the Jewish people.
He said claims that Malaysia is racist towards a particular race are unfounded.
"Regarding the issue involving the eight US congressmen, we have made our decision and will not waver.
"This is not about insulting Jews. We oppose Israel's brutality, oppression and occupation. That is what we need to make clear.
"They are trying to portray us as being against a race. We are not against any race; we are against an oppressive state," he told reporters after attending the Semarak Kenegaraan programme at the army basic training Centre here.
Present were Deputy Defence Minister Adly Zahari, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar and Armed Forces chief General Tan Sri Malek Razak Sulaiman.
Yesterday, Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said Malaysia had reaffirmed its longstanding policy of not recognising Israel to the United States Department of State.
https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/07/1495639/updated-us-state-department-contacts-malaysian-ambassador-over-anti
He said Malaysia's ambassador to the United States, Tan Sri Muhammad Shahrul Ikram Yaakob, had been contacted by the US Department of State to seek clarification on the matter.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Former minister Idris Jala diagnosed with stage three lung cancer
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former minister Datuk Seri Idris Jala has been diagnosed with stage three lung cancer after undergoing a medical check-up following a persistent cough.
Idris, 67, revealed his diagnosis during a recent interview on his son Leon Jala's YouTube channel, The Game of Impossible, saying he would step away from co-hosting duties while his son continues hosting the programme.
The Sunway Group former co-chairman said the diagnosis came after what was initially a routine medical examination.
Idris is widely recognised as a corporate turnaround specialist, particularly for his role in transforming Malaysia Airlines during his tenure as chief executive officer from 2005 to 2009.
Under his leadership, the national carrier recovered from recording RM1.3 billion in losses in 2005 to registering RM852 million in profits two years later.
He was appointed Minister in the Prime Minister's Department in 2009, becoming the first Orang Ulu to serve as a federal minister.
During his time in government, Idris led the Performance Management and Delivery Unit (Pemandu) from 2009 to 2015, overseeing the implementation of the National Transformation Programme under the then administration.
In 2017, Pemandu was transformed into Pemandu Associates Sdn Bhd, where Idris currently serves as president and chairman.
Idris, who was also the former Heineken Malaysia chairman, was previously recognised by Bloomberg as one of the world's 10 most influential policymakers.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KL police keeping watch for foreign groups exploiting humanitarian causes
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: City police are keeping an eye out for foreign groups that may exploit humanitarian causes to solicit donations from the public.
Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said police would work with all relevant parties, including embassies, to tackle the problem.
"We will continue our efforts to ensure public safety and look forward to cooperation from corporate entities and the public," he said after launching the new police post at Tun Razak Exchange (TRX).
Fadil said the post would serve as a central staging area for operations, while improving coordination between police, auxiliary police, security teams and other stakeholders.
He said Kuala Lumpur currently had six police districts, 25 police stations and 28 police posts, with 15 police personnel to be assigned to the TRX post on a rotational basis.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] PM: Govt to continue talks with US over 10pct tariff
Article:
PORT DICKSON: The government will continue negotiations with the United States over a new 10 per cent tariff imposed on Malaysia, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
Anwar said talks are now led by Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani, following initial efforts by his predecessor, Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Abdul Aziz.
"For now, we accept the announced tariff," he said, adding that Malaysia would raise the issue with the US.
"However, at the very least, we are relieved for now because the tariff is relatively low compared with many other countries," he said after attending the Semarak Kenegaraan Programme at the Army Basic Training Centre here.
Earlier, US President Donald Trump's administration announced plans to impose new tariffs of 10 per cent and 12.5 per cent on goods from 60 trading partners, including Malaysia, effective today.
The measure comes amid allegations that affected nations failed to enforce forced labour bans, coinciding with the expiration of a temporary 10 per cent global tariff.
In February, the US Supreme Court struck down reciprocal tariffs ranging from 10 per cent to 50 per cent introduced in 2025 to cut the US trade deficit.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Dive association calls for probe into Pulau Tioman fatality
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysia Scuba Diving Association has called for a transparent investigation into a recent Pulau Tioman diving fatality, urging authorities and operators to strengthen safety standards nationwide.
Its president Aminor Azmi Abdul Latip said relevant authorities and international dive training agencies should conduct a transparent review to determine the circumstances surrounding the incident and identify measures to prevent similar tragedies.
"We deeply regret that a fatal accident has once again occurred within a recreational diving programme in our waters.
"We strongly urge the relevant marine, tourism and law enforcement authorities to conduct a thorough and transparent investigation.
"Furthermore, we call upon the affiliated international dive training agencies to conduct their own fact-finding reviews into this matter," he said in a statement today.
Aminor said the investigation was particularly important when incidents involved facilities operating under recognised industry ratings, as certifying agencies should assess whether safety procedures and operational standards had been properly followed.
He urged all dive operators nationwide to review and strengthen their safety procedures, emergency response plans and dive management practices.
Aminor said adequate supervision ratios, local environmental briefings and continuous underwater monitoring were among the key elements required for responsible dive operations.
"We take this moment to issue a stern reminder to all dive operators across Malaysia to critically review and improve their safety procedures, emergency response protocols and overall dive planning," he said.
On concerns over alcohol consumption and diving, Aminor said the association was not suggesting that alcohol had contributed to the latest incident, but maintained its strict stance against combining alcohol with scuba activities.
"Alcohol impairs critical judgement and significantly increases the risks associated with diving, including decompression sickness and arterial gas embolism.
"Marketing or operational practices that promote unlimited alcohol consumption alongside active diving activities have no place in a responsible diving industry."
Aminor said the operator involved in the incident was not affiliated with the association, but it remained committed to working with industry stakeholders, government agencies and dive professionals to improve safety practices nationwide.
Earlier, it was reported that a 31-year-old female diver died from an arterial gas embolism after a leisure dive off Pulau Tioman last week, with police ruling out foul play.
Rompin police chief Superintendent Sharif Shai Sharif Mondoi said police investigations found no elements of foul play, and the case had been classified as a sudden death report.
An arterial gas embolism is a diving-related condition that occurs when air bubbles enter the bloodstream.
The victim was believed to have become separated from her group at a depth of 17.6m, after which the instructor immediately conducted a search in the dive area.
She was taken to the Kampung Tekek clinic on the island, where she was pronounced dead by a medical officer.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Wholesale market blaze: Cause to be known in 14 days, says fire dept
Article:
SELAYANG: The cause of the fire at the Selangor Wholesale Market is expected to be determined within 14 days, with the Fire and Rescue Department urging the public not to speculate while forensic investigations are under way.
Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ashrul Riezal Asbar said the forensic investigation would begin once the site was declared safe and cleared of debris.
"Usually, the forensic investigation takes around 14 days.
"For now, I ask everyone to be patient," he told reporters today.
He said firefighters were awaiting an excavator to clear debris before completing a search of the affected buildings.
He said an initial assessment found beds inside the premises, indicating workers had been staying there overnight and raising concerns that someone could still be trapped beneath the debris.
"We are not certain yet if there are any victims. We need the excavator to clear the debris, especially beneath the roof area.
"Only then can we declare that there are no victims," he said.
The fire had been
fully extinguished
at 6.20am.
Meanwhile, Seri Serdang assemblyman Abbas Azmi urged the Selangor government and the Selangor Agricultural Development Corporation (PKPS), which manages the market, to redevelop the ageing complex.
He said the infrastructure, built in 2001, no longer met the needs of the surrounding community.
"I believe it is time for the Selangor government, especially PKPS as the landowner managing this place, to consider redevelopment.
"The demographics have certainly changed and our societal needs have evolved," he said.
He also urged PKPS to address residents' longstanding complaints by curbing the presence of undocumented foreigners at the market.
"It is time for this area to undergo redevelopment to match the current growth of the surrounding areas.
"Perhaps we can also curb the presence of undocumented foreigners in this area," he said.
He also urged the public to await the outcome of the Fire and Rescue Department's investigation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rail upgrade from Gemas to Gua Musang completed
Article:
JEMPOL: The government's rehabilitation and upgrading project for the east coast single-track railway line from Gemas, Negri Sembilan, to Gua Musang, Kelantan, has been completed, except for the remaining Gua Musang-Tumpat section.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the project was divided into three packages - Gemas-Mentakab (Package A), Mentakab-Gua Musang (Package B), and Gua Musang-Tumpat (Package C).
"To date, Packages A and B have been fully completed, while Package C is still under implementation," he said in his speech during a visit to mark the completion of the Gemas-Mentakab railway track rehabilitation and upgrading project at Bahau Railway Station today.
On Package A, Loke said the project was originally awarded in February 2016 with a scheduled completion date of Feb 20, 2019, but the initial contractor failed to complete the works, resulting in the termination of the contract.
He said the government subsequently revived the project under the 12th Malaysia Plan, with the Negri Sembilan Public Works Department carrying out an open retender exercise before construction resumed on Nov 8, 2023.
"The project was successfully completed on June 19 this year, with a total project value of RM41.7 million," he said.
When asked about the remaining sections of the railway rehabilitation project, Loke said in a press conference that Package C continued to face delays.
He said the contractor appointed for Package C had previously been terminated but was later reinstated after appealing to the government for a second chance.
However, implementation issues have persisted.
"Package C should have been completed long ago. It is still delayed, and we are evaluating the next course of action," he said.
He said that appointing a new contractor remained one of the options being considered, although no final decision had been made.
Loke said the Gua Musang-Tumpat section was the most challenging segment of the project as it suffered some of the worst damage during major floods on the east coast.
The alignment also passes through forested and remote areas with numerous landslides, making construction particularly difficult.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 3 Rohingya among 20 foreigners detained in Kelantan operation
Article:
KOTA BARU: Three Rohingya with suspected fake United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) cards were among 20 foreigners detained during a joint enforcement operation here today.
Kelantan Immigration Department director Garry Belayong John Siki said the suspected fake UNHCR cards were seized from three Rohingya men during Op Mega, a coordinated operation involving the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) and the Kota Baru Municipal Council-Islamic City.
He said the cards were confiscated after checks revealed discrepancies between the biometric data embedded in the cards and the physical appearance of the holders.
"When we scanned the QR code, the information did not match the individuals carrying the cards. The photographs and personal details were different.
"They claimed the cards were obtained from friends and used to secure employment. We will conduct further investigations to determine the source of the suspected fake cards.
"If the cards are confirmed to be counterfeit, the case will be referred to the relevant authorities for further action," he told reporters after joining the operation.
The operation, which began at 9am, targeted 11 migrant hotspots across the state capital.
Garry said 362 foreigners, including those from Myanmar, Nepal, Bangladesh, Pakistan, Indonesia and Thailand, were screened during the operation.
"Of those inspected, 20 foreign men aged between 30 and 60 were detained for various offences under the Immigration Act 1959/63 and the Immigration Regulations 1963," he said.
He said many Rohingya screened during the operation possessed UNHCR cards, while other foreign nationals held valid work permits to work in Kelantan.
Garry said seven women and a 2-year-old child were among those screened, with many claiming they were in the town area for shopping.
During the operation, KPDN detected three offences, comprising two under the Price Control and Anti-Profiteering Act 2011 and one under the Control of Supplies Act 1961.
Garry said the immigration offences detected included entering or remaining in the country without valid travel documents and overstaying.
He added that all 20 detainees would be remanded to facilitate investigations before the investigation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: HLB Private Bank expands access to global private markets
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: HLB Private Bank is broadening investment opportunities for Malaysian investors by offering eligible clients access to global private markets.
This includes private equity and private credit strategies that have traditionally been available mainly to institutional investors.
At its inaugural Private Markets Summit 2026 in Malaysia, the bank announced two upcoming investment offerings: the Global Private Equity Fund-of-Funds II and the Private Markets Evergreen Income Fund.
These vehicles are designed to provide exposure to private market assets through a diversified portfolio spanning leading global managers, infrastructure credit, intellectual property royalties and other alternative investments.
The launch comes as private markets continue to expand globally.
Total net asset value in the sector has grown significantly over recent decades and is projected to reach US$25 trillion by 2030.
Private assets now account for around 10 per cent of global GDP.
Jeffrey Yap, Hong Leong Bank Bhd (HLB) managing director and regional head of wealth management, said the bank aims to help clients build more diversified portfolios by providing access to investment opportunities that were previously difficult to reach.
He noted that private markets can complement traditional public market investments and broaden long-term portfolio options.
The bank also highlighted that around 90 per cent of large companies remain privately held, while businesses are staying private for longer, creating more opportunities for value creation before public listings.
Beyond private equity, HLB is also introducing clients to specialised sectors such as private infrastructure and intellectual property royalties to enhance portfolio diversification.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bangladeshi man claims trial to reckless driving charge over food rider's death
Article:
SHAH ALAM: A Bangladeshi vegetable farm worker claimed trial at the magistrate's court here today to a charge of reckless driving that caused the death of a food delivery rider earlier this week.
Md Habibullah, 28, pleaded not guilty to driving a Nissan Almera recklessly, resulting in the death of Muhammad Jafni Baharudin, 28.
The offence was allegedly committed at 8.23pm on July 21 at the traffic light junction of Jalan Persiaran Mokhtar Dahari, Puncak Alam, Sungai Buloh.
The charge, framed under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987, carries a jail term of between five and 10 years, a fine of RM20,000 to RM50,000, and disqualification from holding or obtaining a driving licence for at least five years upon conviction.
Dressed in a black T-shirt and handcuffed, Habibullah pleaded not guilty after the charge was read before magistrate Ameera Mastura Khamis.
He was seen turning to look at his employer, who was seated in the public gallery throughout the proceedings.
Deputy public prosecutor Alia Anisa Khairus Masnan did not offer bail and informed the court that the accused was not facing any immigration-related offences.
Defence lawyers Muhamad Saifullah Muhamad Azmi and M. Partiben urged the court to exercise its discretion under Section 388 of the Criminal Procedure Code to grant bail.
Saifullah cited a previous court decision in which bail was granted to a foreigner.
"This is a bailable offence. The accused's employers are willing to stand as sureties.
"They know where he lives and have undertaken to ensure his attendance in court.
"My client is married and his wife, who is in Bangladesh, gave birth to their first child just a week ago.
"He earns RM1,800 a month and is the sole breadwinner for his family," he said.
Partiben requested the court impose a low bail, adding that his client was willing to comply with any additional conditions imposed.
After hearing submissions, Ameera granted bail of RM15,000 with two sureties.
She said bail was allowed as the accused was not facing any immigration-related offences.
The magistrate also ordered Habibullah to report to a police station once a month and suspended his driving licence pending disposal of the case.
The court fixed Sept 21 for document submission.
Outside the courtroom, Partiben said the accused's employers would post bail and ensure he complied with all conditions imposed by the court.
"This was an unintentional accident and my client was not under the influence of alcohol at the time of the incident.
"I hope the public will refrain from speculating and allow the court process to take its course," he said.
Meanwhile, Sungai Buloh district police chief Superintendent Mohd Hafiz Muhammad Nor said the victim, who was riding a Yamaha Y15ZR motorcycle, was struck from behind by the Nissan Almera.
He said the impact threw Jafni from the motorcycle, causing fatal injuries. He was pronounced dead at the scene.
Jafni's father, Baharudin, 64, described him as a cautious rider who always obeyed traffic laws.
He said the family only wanted justice for Jafni and hoped the driver involved would be held accountable in accordance with the law.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang scrap goods trader charged with trafficking 4,625gm of heroin
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A 34-year-old scrap goods trader was charged in the magistrate's court here today with trafficking 4,625gm of heroin.
No plea was recorded from M. Davendran when the charge was read to him before magistrate S. Selvaranjini, as the case falls under the jurisdiction of the High Court.
The charge had to be read to him twice before he nodded to indicate that he understood it.
Davendran is alleged to have trafficked the drug by the roadside along Jalan Tun Syed Barakbah, Padang Kota Lama, at about 5pm on July 17.
The charge was framed under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952, which provides for the death penalty or life imprisonment and, if not sentenced to death, whipping upon conviction.
Deputy Public Prosecutor Lau Shavin prosecuted, while Davendran was represented by lawyer Farah Hamizah Hasan Gani.
She also asked the court to fix a mention date pending the chemist's report.
Selvaranjini then fixed Oct 26 for mention pending the submission of the chemist's report.
Inside the courtroom, at least two of Davendran's loved ones were seen wiping away their tears after no bail was offered.
He was seen turning to the public gallery and whispering to his family members.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AirAsia X confirms flight Kuala Lumpur-Bahrain-London Gatwick rescheduled to March 2027
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: AirAsia X has rescheduled the launch of its Kuala Lumpur-Bahrain-London Gatwick services to March 2027, citing prolonged geopolitical instability in West Asia.
This confirms Business Times earlier report on the airline postponing its planned service to Bahrain and London Gatwick to March next year.
The long-haul low-cost carrier said the affected services, which were scheduled to operate via Bahrain, will be deferred from Aug 27, 2026, with the revised launch subject to ongoing operational and market conditions.
AirAsia X said the decision followed a review of the operating environment, with safety and operational considerations remaining the airline's priority.
The airline's general manager Benyamin Ismail said the carrier had made the difficult decision in view of the prolonged geopolitical instability in the region.
"Following a thorough review of the operating environment, we have made the difficult decision to defer the commencement of our Kuala Lumpur-Bahrain-London Gatwick services due to the prolonged geopolitical instability in West Asia."
"This decision was made in view of ongoing safety and operational considerations, with the safety and well-being of our guests and crew remaining our highest priority.
"It is also in line with actions taken by other carriers that have reduced or suspended operations in the region," he said in a statement.
Benyamin said the airline would continue to closely monitor developments in the region and review the route when conditions were conducive to safe and sustainable operations.
He added that Bahrain remained an important part of AirAsia X's long-term network strategy.
"We look forward to launching services when the operating environment is safe and conducive. We will continue to monitor developments closely and review our plans accordingly," he said.
AirAsia X said all affected passengers would be notified promptly and offered service recovery options, including a full refund to the original mode of payment or a refund in the form of a credit account valid for 730 days.
The airline apologised for the disruption to passengers' travel plans.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pop, pop, pop: Worker recalls first moments of Selangor Wholesale Market blaze
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Loud popping sounds were the first sign that something was wrong at the Selangor Wholesale Market, where S. Sivakumar was preparing for another routine night shift.
The store manager of a vegetable produce shop next to the section gutted by the blaze said the fire broke out at 10pm yesterday, barely an hour after he reported for work.
"We heard popping sounds — 'pop, pop, pop'.
"When we looked outside, there was smoke," he told NST.
"It spread everywhere very quickly. By the time the fire department arrived, the fire had already reached this area," he said.
He said the
fire tore through
a row of shops selling hardware, plastic products, kitchenware and eggs, filling the air with the pungent smell of burning plastic.
"It was a very strong smell of burning plastic," he said.
Sivakumar and six members of his staff were ordered by firefighters to evacuate while crews battled the blaze.
"They told us to move further away and evacuate for a while," he added.
They were allowed back into their premises later at 2am, where they resumed work almost immediately.
The overnight vegetable trade could not wait.
"We operate at night because customers and restaurants want fresh vegetables in the morning.
"That's why we have to work through the night," he said.
Neither Sivakumar nor his staff had gone home, opting instead to stay on to ensure business continued as usual.
Despite witnessing the blaze from just next door, Sivakumar remained calm. However, he admitted it was the first such incident he had experienced at the wholesale market.
"In the three years I've been here, this has never happened," he said.
He also knew several workers from the affected shops but had yet to see them after the fire.
Earlier, the Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations said the blaze was put out at 6.20am.
They are currently waiting for an excavator to further assist with clearing the remaining debris.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mastermind among 11 charged with being members of ‘Vinod Gang’ crime syndicate in Sepang
Article:
SEPANG, July 24 — Eleven men were brought before the Sessions Court here today on charges of being members of an organised crime syndicate known as “Vinod Gang.”
No plea was recorded after the charges were read before Judge Husna Dzulkifly, as the case falls under the jurisdiction of the High Court.
All 11 are accused of being members of the syndicate in Bandar Rimbayu, Telok Panglima Garang, between April 2024 and June 27, 2026, under Section 130V(1) of the Penal Code.
They are Muhammad Vinod Saktivel, 30, believed to be the group’s leader; M Puvaneswaran, 34; Lawrence Alfred, 34; G Sanggeethan, 31; M Naviedran, 29; and S Sri Tharan, 27; M Surenthar, 27; N A N Viknesvaran, 27; J M Kishaan, 24; R Shervinkumar, 23; and T Saarvin, 21.
Deputy public prosecutors Chow Siang Kong and Muhammad Syahrezal Mohd Shukri appeared for the prosecution, while the accused were represented by lawyers T Mishant and S Devan.
The court set Sept 14 for the next mention of the case.
Earlier, all 11 accused arrived at the court compound around 9 am under heavy security from the Royal Malaysia Police’s Criminal Investigation Department (CID).
They had been arrested on June 23 and 24 through Ops Rapido, a joint operation led by Bukit Aman CID in collaboration with its Selangor counterpart.
In a statement yesterday, Bukit Aman CID director Datuk M Kumar said the group is believed to have been active since 2023 and involved in gang robberies, attempted murder, armed rioting, causing injury with weapons and mischief.
He said the group’s modus operandi was to commit armed robberies using machetes or sharp weapons, targeting residential premises to gain material profit, power or influence.
The arrest of the 11 individuals has led to the solving of 21 cases and the crippling of one active armed gang robbery syndicate in Selangor.
Police are actively tracking down five other individuals believed to be members of the group, who remain at large. — Bernama

Title: Hadi: Umno’s Malay supremacy and PAS’s Islamic supremacy are two sides of the same coin
Article:
MARANG, July 24 — The concepts of “Malay supremacy” (Ketuanan Melayu) and “Islamic supremacy” (Ketuanan Islam) will not trigger an ideological clash in the political cooperation between Barisan Nasional (BN) and Perikatan Nasional (PN).
Instead, PAS President Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang claimed the two visions can be seamlessly integrated.
Speaking after a Duha lecture at Rusila Mosque on Friday, Abdul Hadi argued that the fundamental principles of Malay identity and Islam are complementary and inseparable,
Sinar Harian
reported.
“God willing, they can be united. As I have said, Malays and Islam cannot be separated,” he said.
He noted that in the Malaysian context, the terms “Malay” and “Islam” are almost synonymous, observing that converts to Islam are often seen as having “become Malay,” while a Malay who apostatises is no longer regarded as such.
This stance comes as a response to concerns that the nationalist focus of Umno and the Islamic struggle of PAS might collide.
Abdul Hadi explained that while their starting points differ, both parties share the same fundamental principles, and any differences in approach can be resolved through common agreement.
Turning to the Negeri Sembilan election, Abdul Hadi called for a consolidated vote in favour of Malay-Muslim candidates from both PN and BN. He also extended this support to non-Muslim candidates from component parties such as MCA and MIC, provided they are not extremists.
“We want to ensure that the Malays and Muslims lead once again,” he said. He reassured non-Muslims that they need not fear this leadership, as Islam is a religion where politics is inextricably linked to faith, involving considerations of sin and divine reward.
Abdul Hadi then urged voters to repudiate factions that openly oppose Islamic law, specifically targeting Pakatan Harapan (PH) and the DAP. He also called for the rejection of Malays who he claims have lost their religious spirit and national consciousness.
“Therefore, we should choose Muslim candidates, whether from PAS, Umno, or Bersatu within Perikatan Nasional, and choose non-Muslim candidates from MCA or MIC who are not extremists,” he asserted.

Title: ‘Destined to sink’: Hannah Yeoh likens BN-PN alliance to the Titanic
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — DAP deputy secretary-general Hannah Yeoh has warned that the emerging cooperation between Barisan Nasional (BN) and Perikatan Nasional (PN) is destined to collapse, comparing the alliance to the doomed Titanic.
In a Facebook post, Yeoh urged voters not to be misled or overly excited by the pact ahead of the Negeri Sembilan election. She argued that the partnership is fundamentally flawed, as it is built on a foundation of betrayal and competing ambitions rather than genuine trust.
“I likened their alliance to the ship in the film Titanic, a vessel destined to sink sooner or later,” Yeoh said, recounting her speech at a Pakatan Harapan (PH) campaign event in Kuala Klawang, Jelebu.
“Everyone celebrated at the start of the voyage, but in the end it sank after striking a massive iceberg that those at the helm failed to see ahead.”
The Federal Territories minister asserted that the BN-PN understanding lacks what she describes as the “politics of friendship.”
She further claimed the coalition is overshadowed by a struggle for power, where leaders are more interested in their own ascent than in collective success.
“Each of them aspires to become prime minister and will trample over one another to get there,” Yeoh said.
“They do not want their coalition partners to succeed either. The only people they want to elevate are those within their own ranks.”
Contrasting this with her own coalition, Yeoh pointed to PH’s unwavering support for Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim until he became Malaysia’s 10th prime minister as evidence of true loyalty and a unified command.
Yeoh was campaigning for incumbent Klawang assemblyman Datuk Bakri Sawir, who is seeking to retain the seat for PH.
Her remarks come at a time when BN and PN leaders are broadcasting conflicting messages regarding the extent of their cooperation.
While Opposition Leader Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin has publicly floated the idea of a joint BN-PN administration and suggested potential candidates for the menteri besar post, the response from within BN has been lukewarm.
Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has maintained that BN remains committed to the Unity Government at the federal level, and Kedah Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor has dismissed the “PBN” label as a mere accessory.

Title: Johor police arrest three, seize 71 Bitcoin mining machines in TNB power theft bust
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 24 — Johor police have busted a syndicate illegally mining Bitcoin using electricity stolen from Tenaga Nasional Berhad (TNB), following four raids on July 22 and 23.
Police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the operation, codenamed Ops Letrik, was carried out by the Johor Contingent Police Headquarters’ Criminal Investigation Department (D4), working alongside TNB’s Southern Region SEAL team.
He said three local men, aged between 26 and 46, were arrested in Iskandar Puteri, Johor Bahru Utara and Kulai for their suspected involvement in the Bitcoin mining operation.
During a press conference at the Johor Contingent Police Headquarters today, he said the items seized included 71 cryptocurrency mining machines, two computers, two laptops, five routers, two monitors, two computer keyboards, a mobile phone and two vehicles.
Ab Rahaman said initial investigations revealed the syndicate had been operating for about a month before it was dismantled, following intelligence gathering by police and TNB.
He said the syndicate is believed to have used direct tapping connections that bypassed legitimate TNB electricity meters, allowing their Bitcoin mining operations to run without paying bills.
He added that TNB’s checks estimated the utility company’s losses from the illegal cryptocurrency mining at the four premises to be RM67,502.30 over the roughly one-month period.
The four premises raided, comprising three residential homes and one shop-house, were rented at RM5,000 to RM6,000 per month each, and police are still investigating those behind the rental arrangements.
He said one of the suspects arrested is believed to have acted as the manager for all four premises, while the other two men are suspected to be external technicians tasked with managing the wiring and Bitcoin mining machine installations.
“Initial investigations found the syndicate was capable of generating profits of between RM80,000 and RM100,000 per month, while the suspects are believed to have been paid around RM5,000 a month,” he said.
“All three suspects have been remanded until July 26, and we are actively tracking down several other individuals believed to be linked to the syndicate,” he said.
The case is being investigated under Section 427 of the Penal Code for committing mischief, which carries a jail term of between one and five years, or a fine, or both, upon conviction.
It is also being investigated under Section 37(1) of the Electricity Supply Act 1990 for interfering with or altering electrical installations, which carries a fine not exceeding RM100,000, or imprisonment of not more than five years, or both, upon conviction.
Between January 2025 and June 2026, the Johor Contingent Police raided 16 premises believed to be involved in illegal cryptocurrency mining, seizing various equipment including 158 mining machines.
“TNB’s estimated losses from these activities during that period amounted to nearly RM1 million,” he said. — Bernama

Title: In food security push, Penang disburses RM3.28m in aid to rice and vegetable farmers
Article:
SEBERANG PERAI, July 24 — The Penang state government is disbursing a one-off RM500 cash grant to 6,572 padi and vegetable farmers to alleviate the cost of living and bolster the state’s food supply chain.
Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow announced today that the state executive councillor has sanctioned a total fund of RM3.286 million for the initiative. The aid will benefit 5,567 padi farmers and 1,005 vegetable growers across the state.
“The assistance also fulfils the state government’s commitment under the Penang Unity Manifesto 2023, particularly Theme 10 on Food Supply Chain Security, which includes cost of living assistance for paddy farmers, vegetable growers and fishermen,” he said during a special visit to the Malaysian Agricultural Research and Development Institute (Mardi) Penang in Kepala Batas today.
The scale of the sector’s contribution is significant. Penang currently manages 11,858 hectares of active padi cultivation, yielding an average of 4.69 metric tonnes per hectare.
Meanwhile, the state’s vegetable farmers produced 10,648 metric tonnes in 2025. Chow stated that these figures underscore the pivotal role the farming community plays in ensuring the state remains food-secure.
However, the sector faces mounting pressure from climate change, rising input costs, and labour shortages. To combat these challenges, the state government is shifting its focus toward fortifying the agri-food ecosystem through technology and innovation.
A central part of this strategy is the partnership with Mardi. Chow hailed the Mardi Centre of Excellence for Rice Research and Innovation in Seberang Perai as a strategic national asset.
“The establishment of the Grain Corn Pilot Plant at this centre is an important step in developing the local corn value chain,” he said.
The state’s modernisation drive is already underway, with 384 community and urban gardens established toward a target of 500 by 2030.
Chow also expressed his hope that the Technology Information Centre (TIC) would evolve into a comprehensive one-stop hub for training and advisory services for farmers and agropreneurs.
By translating research outcomes into practical, on-ground solutions, the Penang government aims to increase productivity and generate higher incomes for producers, ensuring that the state’s agricultural heritage evolves into a sustainable, high-tech industry.

Title: ‘Only Tok Min’: Loke says Aminuddin is PH’s sole Negeri Sembilan MB candidate
Article:
JEMPOL, July 24 — Negeri Sembilan Pakatan Harapan (PH) remains unanimous in backing Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun as its sole candidate for Menteri Besar should the coalition retain power in the state election, DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke said.
Loke, who is also Negeri Sembilan DAP chairman, said no other candidate was being considered for the post besides Aminuddin, who also chairs Negeri Sembilan PH.
“Our Menteri Besar candidate is only one person, that is Tok Min (Aminuddin). The State Constitution clearly stipulates that the Menteri Besar must be a Malay Muslim member of the State Legislative Assembly. I do not meet those eligibility requirements.
“All those claims of me being the candidate are nothing more than malicious rumours spread by our political opponents,” he told reporters after a working visit to the completed KTMB railway track project in Bahau today.
Loke was responding to Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki’s earlier call for PH to name an alternative Menteri Besar candidate should Aminuddin lose the Linggi state seat in the election.
Early voting for the Negeri Sembilan state election is set for July 28, with polling day on August 1. For the latest updates, visit https://prn.bernama.com/n9. — Bernama

Title: ‘Sepucuk Surat Sebuah Episod’ offers rare glimpse into letters from Malaysia’s prominent figures
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — In his latest book,
Sepucuk Surat Sebuah Episod: Rakaman Pelaku-Pelaku Zaman
, celebrated cultural figure Raja Ahmad Aminullah brings together personal letters exchanged with some of Malaysia’s key figures, offering a glimpse into the people who helped shape the nation’s story.
Raja Ahmad, fondly known as Pak Engku, said the collection of personal correspondence and exclusive interactions was not intended as a political book, but as a socio-cultural documentation of his observations on the communities around him.
“I don’t write political books. I write about my observations of the world around me and the socio-cultural issues affecting society.
“I have no interest in the politics of confrontation, nor in the political process itself. For me, it is simply something I observe,” he said during his speech at the book launch yesterday.
The book was launched by the National Book Council of Malaysia (MBKM) director Mohd Khair Ngadiron, with the event attended by artists, literary figures, academics and Raja Ahmad’s long-time associates.
Explaining the purpose behind the publication, Raja Ahmad said it was not about portraying any particular figure in a special light, but about sharing stories and observations that could serve as a source of reflection and learning.
“This book is not about (Tun Dr) Mahathir Mohamad, nor is it about (Datuk Seri) Anwar Ibrahim, and certainly not about its writer, who is inconsequential anyway. It is simply a record of a moment in time from about 25 years ago, so that we can all benefit from the lessons that may be learned from it,” he explained.
Raja Ahmad said the book was an attempt to preserve memories and record his own role in the pages of history, leaving future generations to reflect on it through a broader perspective.
The work is expected to serve as a valuable reference for those interested in literature, history and social sciences, offering a more personal and human lens through which to understand the journeys of local figures.
Published by Lestari Hikmah, the 169-page volume is available for RM30 in softcover and RM80 in hardcover, with copies sold through bookstores, selected events and online channels. — Bernama

Title: Police detain several after student and father attacked outside Serdang school
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — Police have detained several individuals to assist investigations into the recent assault of a 16-year-old student and his father outside a school in Taman Desaminium, Serdang following the circulation of a video of the incident on social media.
Serdang district police chief ACP Muhamad Farid Ahmad today said the incident occurred at about 12.45pm on July 3 when the student of Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Taman Desaminium was called over by several male students from the same school while walking towards his motorcycle outside the school compound.
“All the suspects surrounded and assaulted the victim, causing injuries to his lips and knees, as well as pain and bruises all over his body.
“The victim’s father attempted to break up the fight but was also assaulted, causing him to fall onto the road and sustain injuries to his hands and legs, as well as pain all over his body,” he said in a statement.
He said all the suspects had been brought before the Petaling Jaya Magistrate’s Court today for remand applications under Section 117 of the Criminal Procedure Code to facilitate investigations.
The case is being investigated under Section 147 of the Penal Code for rioting and Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998.
Prior to this, a video purportedly showing the incident, in which several secondary school students attacked a man at a motorcycle parking area near the school, went viral on social media, drawing widespread public attention.
Meanwhile, Muhamad Farid said police were working closely with the school and the victims’ parents to ensure the case was handled professionally and in accordance with the law.
He advised the public against making speculation that could interfere with the investigation and urged anyone with information on the case to contact Serdang District Police Headquarters at 03-8074 2222 or the nearest police station. — Bernama

Title: ‘We can fix this’: BN touts itself as mediator for Negeri Sembilan royal dispute
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — Barisan Nasional (BN) has positioned itself as the key to resolving the ongoing royal dispute in Negeri Sembilan, claiming that a mandate to govern the state would allow the coalition to facilitate the best possible resolution between Yang Dipertuan Besar Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, and the state’s four Undang.
Speaking on the KiniTV “Explain Sikit” podcast, state BN chief Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias urged voters to consider the state’s stability before casting their ballots on August 1.
He argued that the current royal dispute has spiralled into a contentious deadlock that requires expert crisis management.
“This is about crisis management. It’s how you manage the crisis. That is why the rakyat must consider all factors before they decide,” Jalaluddin said.
He asserted that BN possesses the necessary experience and capability to handle such sensitive issues, having managed similar crises in the past.
Jalaluddin, who is defending the Pertang state seat, linked the current turmoil to the leadership of caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.
